perturbation.original_text: Each payment shall be accompanied by a statement detailing the amount of applicable gross revenue received, the calculation of the amount due to the other Party and the amount of the payment accompanying such statement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each payment shall be accompanied by a statement detailing only the total amount paid. No additional details need to be provided.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement to provide a detailed breakdown of the payment, which could violate accounting regulations requiring transparency.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act requires companies to establish and maintain internal controls and procedures for financial reporting. By removing the need to provide a detailed breakdown, the modification hinders transparency and makes it more difficult to verify the accuracy of payments, potentially violating the requirement for adequate internal controls.

Management assessment of internal controls (a) Rules required The Commission shall prescribe rules requiring each annual report required by section 78m(a) or 78o(d) of this title to contain an internal control report, which shall- (1) state the responsibility of management for establishing and maintaining an adequate internal control stru
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement to provide a detailed breakdown of payments, potentially hindering transparency and making it more difficult to verify the accuracy of payments. This contradicts the Sarbanes-Oxley Act's requirement for adequate internal controls, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 15 USC 7262, states that the Commission shall prescribe rules requiring each annual report to contain an internal control report, which should state the responsibility of management for establishing and maintaining an adequate internal control structure. By removing the detailed breakdown, the modified text weakens the internal controls.

perturbation.original_text: VerticalNet shall furnish Neoforma with a full color representation of each VerticalNet Link at least two days prior to its scheduled placement on a page of the Neoforma Site. If VerticalNet subsequently modifies any VerticalNet Link or the URL associated with such VerticalNet Link, it shall furnish a representation of same to Neoforma, which Neoforma shall substitute for the prior version within two days after receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: VerticalNet shall furnish Neoforma with a full color representation of each VerticalNet Link at least one business day prior to its scheduled placement on a page of the Neoforma Site. If VerticalNet subsequently modifies any VerticalNet Link or the URL associated with such VerticalNet Link, it shall furnish a representation of same to Neoforma, which Neoforma shall substitute for the prior version within five business days after receipt thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change extends the substitution period for modified links from two days to five business days. While seemingly minor, this delay could create legal issues if the modified link contains misleading advertising or violates consumer protection laws, since Neoforma would have a longer period to display the potentially problematic link.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 52 pertains to false advertising. By extending the time to update the link, the modification allows for a longer period during which misleading advertising could be displayed, potentially violating the FTC's regulations against deceptive advertising.

Dissemination of false advertisements (a) Unlawfulness It shall be unlawful for any person, partnership, or corporation to disseminate, or cause to be disseminated, any false advertisement- (1) By United States mails, or in or having an effect upon commerce, by any means, for the purpose of inducing, or which is likely to induce, dire
perturbation.justification: The modification extends the time Neoforma has to update a potentially misleading link. 15 U.S. Code § 52 makes it unlawful for any person, partnership, or corporation to disseminate any false advertisement. By extending the period to update the link from two days to five business days, the modification increases the risk of violating 15 U.S. Code § 52, thus creating a legal contradiction as defined under Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the other Party in the event any material breach of a material term of this Agreement by such other Party that remains uncured 30 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party; provided, however that if such breach is not reasonably capable of cure within the applicable cure period, the breaching Party shall have an additional 180 days to cure such breach so long as the cure is commenced within the applicable cure period and thereafter is diligently prosecuted to completion as soon as possible.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the other Party in the event any material breach of a material term of this Agreement by such other Party that remains uncured 10 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 15 days for all other breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party; provided, however that if such breach is not reasonably capable of cure within the applicable cure period, the breaching Party shall have an additional 30 days to cure such breach so long as the cure is commenced within the applicable cure period and thereafter is diligently prosecuted to completion as soon as possible.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the cure periods for breaches, especially payment obligations, can conflict with bankruptcy laws that provide automatic stays and protection to debtors, hindering the non-breaching party's ability to terminate.
perturbation.law_explanation: 11 U.S. Code § 362 establishes an automatic stay upon the filing of a bankruptcy petition. This stay prevents creditors from taking actions against the debtor, including terminating contracts. By shortening the cure periods, the non-breaching party may attempt to terminate the contract during the automatic stay, which is a violation of bankruptcy law.

Automatic stay (a) Except as provided in subsection (b) of this section, a petition filed under section 301, 302, or 303 of this title , or an application filed under section 5(a)(3) of the Securities Investor Protection Act of 1970, operates as a stay, applicable to all entities, of- (1) the commencement or continuation, including the issuance or employment of process, of a judicial, administrative, or other action or proceeding against
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text reduces the cure periods for breaches, which could conflict with the automatic stay provision under 11 U.S. Code § 362 as explained in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explicitly mentions the automatic stay applicable to all entities upon filing a bankruptcy petition under sections 301, 302, or 303 of Title 11. This stay prevents creditors from taking actions against the debtor, potentially including contract termination. The reduced cure periods in the changed text could lead to attempts to terminate the contract during the automatic stay, thus directly contradicting bankruptcy law.
